summary.intent: Implement tasks #297, #298, #299 — the framework-agnostic core of 8 color components (ColorSwatch, ColorField, ColorArea, ColorSlider, ColorWheel, AngleSlider, ColorSwatchPicker) plus a shared ColorValue type in the ars-ui Rust component library, then drive the implementation through Codex review to approval.
summary.outcome: All 8 components and the ColorValue foundation were implemented, PR #698 opened with CI green. Three full Codex review passes (20 findings fixed across 4 rounds) were completed; the 4th round (2 readonly-mid-drag findings) was in the process of being committed when the transcript ended.

repo: core_maths::CoreFloat is the workspace's established no_std float tool; use the fully-qualified form CoreFloat::method(x,...) matching ars-i18n convention. CoreFloat lacks min/max — need private fmin/fmax helpers.
MessageFn has a fixed set of From<F> impls per argument signature; adding a new color message signature requires a new impl block in crates/ars-core/src/message_fn.rs.

Drag pattern: the adapter owns pointer listeners and fires DragMove/DragEnd into the machine; the core fires a typed Effect::ChangeEnd on DragEnd to invoke on_change_end. The spec's use_platform_effects()/track_pointer_drag() in core do not exist.

on_props_changed + SyncValue/SetProps events are required on every stateful machine so that controlled value/disabled/readonly/orientation prop updates apply after mount. Without them, prop changes go stale until remount.

Bindable<T>::get() returns the controlled prop value; Bindable::pending() (added during review) returns the internally-set value — needed so drag on_change_end reports the just-dragged value in controlled mode.
snapshot-count xci-fast gate requires at least one snapshot per declared state variant; missing a state causes the gate to fail.

CI native coverage runs as 'cargo +nightly llvm-cov nextest --branch --workspace --lcov'; using -p instead of --workspace underestimates ars-components branch coverage by ~1.1% because cross-crate integration tests are excluded.
ars-components per-crate coverage threshold is 90% line and 90% branch (checked by 'cargo xtask coverage check-all'). Branch coverage requires both arms of one-armed if-blocks to be exercised.
CssProperty::Custom takes the property name without the -- prefix; passing --ars-swatch-color would render as ----ars-swatch-color.
Part enum variants containing f64 (e.g. Marker { value: f64 }) cannot use #[derive(ComponentPart)] because f64 is not Eq or Hash; requires a manual ComponentPart impl using to_bits() for hashing.
Colors should use rem_euclid(360.0) for hue wrapping, not %; % preserves negative remainders and can produce negative hues on large decrements.
strip_prefix('#').unwrap_or(hex) strips exactly one leading #; trim_start_matches('#') strips all leading # characters, silently accepting ##3366ff.
ColorValue::new wraps hue into [0,360) — a load-bearing invariant for to_rgb sextant math. Hue slider needs a separate slider_value field (unwrapped 0..=max) to make the 360° endpoint reachable.

code.finding: from_hex byte-slices &hex[0..2] after matching on byte length 6/8; non-ASCII input like #ああ is 6 UTF-8 bytes but 2 chars, causing a panic at non-char-boundary. Fixed by guarding with hex.is_ascii() before byte indexing.

parse_rgb_args/parse_hsl_args/parse_hsb_args accepted NaN and infinity because parse::<f64>() succeeds on those strings; ColorValue::new only debug_asserts finiteness. Fixed with a parse_finite_f64 helper that returns None unless is_finite().

format_value shows percentage channels as integers (e.g. 50 for saturation 0.5), but commit_input parsed 50 and clamped to [0,1], silently changing the value to 1.0. Committed input must be divided by 100 for percentage channels.

SetProps updated channel/color_format but left ctx.input_text in the old representation; since SyncValue runs before SetProps, it formatted with the old channel. Fixed by reformatting input_text from the current value with the new channel/format when not focused.

Props::readonly only set aria-readonly but not native readonly; the machine still accepted Change and blur commit_input while readonly. Fixed by setting HtmlAttr::ReadOnly and returning None from Change/blur commits when ctx.readonly.

On initial render focused_index is None; roving logic gave every item tabindex=-1, making the listbox unreachable by keyboard. Fixed with a tabbable_index() helper that makes the selected/first item tabindex=0 in idle state.

aria-valuetext announced only raw channel numbers; spec §3.1 requires a human-readable color name. Fixed by threading color_name_en() into the value_text message. Also, value_text was multiplying every channel by 100 including hue, producing announcements like 'hue 18000%'; fixed with channel-aware per-axis reading strings.

Hidden form input stayed enabled when the control was disabled, allowing form submission. Fixed by mirroring the disabled prop onto the hidden input's HtmlAttr::Disabled. Same issue existed in color_area, color_wheel, angle_slider, color_field, color_swatch_picker.
SetProps updated min/max but left ctx.value, so an out-of-range value persisted in aria-valuenow and the hidden input. Fixed by re-clamping ctx.value to the new bounds inside SetProps.

Bindable<T> stores both an internal value (set by set()) and a controlled value (returned by get()). Added pending() method that returns the internally-set value — needed so drag change-end effects report the just-dragged color in controlled mode rather than the stale controlled prop.

Wheel thumb set aria-labelledby to a *-label IDREF but ColorWheel has no Label part, creating a dangling reference. Fixed by dropping aria-labelledby (aria-label is already set). ColorSlider does have a Label part and correctly retains aria-labelledby.

workflow: Spec-sync work (mirroring implementation changes back into spec markdown files) was delegated to a persistent subagent to protect the main context window; the agent received exact instructions about what changed and ran 'cargo xtask spec validate' to verify.

The waiting-for-codex-review skill drives a poll loop checking for 👀 (Codex started) → 👍 (approved) or unresolved threads (new findings). Each finding requires a TDD test written first, then the fix, then reply + resolve the thread before re-triggering '@codex review'.
cargo xci-fast (10 gates: fmt, check, clippy, unit, integration, adapter, spec-compile-snippets, error-variant-coverage, mutual-exclusion, snapshot-count) must pass before any push.
snapshot-reviewed label must be re-applied after any commit that adds or updates .snap fixtures.

When measuring branch coverage locally, use the exact CI command ('cargo +nightly llvm-cov nextest --branch --workspace') plus 'cargo xtask coverage check-all'; the -p flag underestimates and gives a false-passing result.
Using 'status' as a variable name in zsh background scripts crashes them — 'status' is a read-only shell special in zsh.

friction: Codex was rate-limited after the first @codex review trigger ('You have reached your Codex usage limits for code reviews'), blocking the review loop for an unknown period.
zsh read-only 'status' variable crashed two background watch scripts before being renamed to 'cov_state'.

Coverage CI failed after the initial push (ars-components branch 89.7% < 90% threshold) because one-armed if-flag conditionals in *_attrs methods were only exercised on one arm. Also uncovered a dead branch: color_field's has_description flag had no setter.

pre-existing pagination/mod.rs clippy lint ('unnecessary_option_map_or_else') surfaced on stable 1.96 because the workspace denies clippy::suspicious; required an unrelated 1-line fix before xci-fast could pass.

The spec's use_platform_effects()/track_pointer_drag() drag APIs are referenced in 4 component specs but do not exist in the codebase; required full reconciliation of the drag pattern across all 4 specs.

Spec drift was pervasive: color_name_parts arity, ColorValue module path, Callback typing, CssProperty::Custom prefix behavior, and AngleSlider's manual ComponentPart all diverged between spec and real codebase.
TransitionPlan closure type inference (E0282) cascaded through every early-return closure in color_field, requiring explicit '|ctx: &mut Context|' annotations throughout.
Service::new panics on empty Props.id — not documented anywhere obvious; discovered only when tests failed.
open_items: Codex 👍 approval is still pending (4th round of readonly-mid-drag fixes were being committed at transcript end); PR #698 cannot merge until Codex approves and user gives the go-ahead.
Issues #297/#298/#299 close automatically on merge (via Closes keywords in PR body) but only after CI green + Codex 👍 + user merge decision.

Adapter specs (leptos/dioxus-components/specialized/color-*.md) describe framework-specific adapter components for all 8 color controls — explicitly out of scope per the issues, remaining for a future task.

ColorValue type is currently documented inside color-picker.md §Types but logically belongs in a dedicated spec/shared/color-types.md; promotion would touch manifest + 8 frontmatters and was deferred as orthogonal to impl correctness.
block v0.1.6 future-incompat warning from a transitive dependency — pre-existing, not caused by this work, but will eventually need resolution.

The spec's compile-snippet harness concatenates cross-block symbols via shims; the color machine blocks are marked no_check because they reference external symbols. A future improvement could make them individually compilable.
